name: Lewis Tunstall
bio: Lewis Tunstall is a Machine Learning Engineer at Hugging Face, where he leads post-training efforts and builds open-source tools to align language models with human preferences. He co-authored the O'Reilly bestseller "Natural Language Processing with Transformers" (2022) with Leandro von Werra and Thomas Wolf, and is a core contributor to TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) and the Alignment Handbook. Before transitioning to ML, Tunstall earned a PhD in Theoretical Physics from the University of Adelaide, was a 2010 Fulbright Scholar, and held research positions in Australia, the USA, and Switzerland (University of Bern). He grew up in northwest Tasmania, Australia and is now based in Bern, Switzerland.

timeline.event: Awarded inaugural Fulbright South Australia Scholarship; PhD candidate at University of Adelaide in Theoretical Physics (particle physics)
Visiting researcher at Higgs Centre for Theoretical Physics, University of Edinburgh; affiliated with University of Bern ITP
Conceived idea for NLP with Transformers book; partnered with Leandro von Werra and sent cold email to Thomas Wolf at Hugging Face
Co-created TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) library for fine-tuning LLMs with RLHF
Joined Hugging Face as Machine Learning Engineer; co-developed Hugging Face Course with Sylvain Gugger and Lysandre Jik
Published "Natural Language Processing with Transformers" (O'Reilly) with Leandro von Werra and Thomas Wolf
Released SetFit: Efficient Few-Shot Learning Without Prompts (arXiv:2209.11055)
Released Zephyr-7B: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment — surpassed Llama2-Chat-70B on MT-Bench using dDPO without human annotation (arXiv:2310.16944)
Published Alignment Handbook: robust open-source recipes to align language models with human and AI preferences
Won first AI Math Olympiad (AIMO) progress prize with NuminaMath — 850k math problem-solution pairs dataset
Released SmolLM2-Instruct alignment recipe via Alignment Handbook
Co-authored SmolLM3-3B: small multilingual long-context reasoner with Anchored Preference Optimization post-training
Co-launched Open-R1 initiative to reproduce DeepSeek R1 distillation pipeline; released OlympicCoder for competitive programming

key_contributions.title: Natural Language Processing with Transformers (O'Reilly)
key_contributions.description: Bestselling book co-authored with Leandro von Werra and Thomas Wolf, providing a hands-on guide to building NLP applications with Hugging Face Transformers. Revised edition published in full color.

key_contributions.title: TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning)
key_contributions.description: Core contributor to Hugging Face's library for fine-tuning and aligning language models via RLHF, DPO, and other preference learning methods. One of the most widely used alignment libraries in the open-source ecosystem.

key_contributions.title: Zephyr-7B
key_contributions.description: Led the Zephyr project demonstrating that distilled DPO (dDPO) with AI feedback can produce a 7B-parameter chat model surpassing Llama2-Chat-70B on MT-Bench, without requiring human annotation.

key_contributions.title: Alignment Handbook
key_contributions.description: Open-source collection of robust recipes to align language models with human and AI preferences, covering SFT, DPO, Constitutional AI, and model-specific fine-tuning guides.

key_contributions.title: SetFit
key_contributions.description: Efficient few-shot learning framework that fine-tunes Sentence Transformers contrastively on small labeled sets, achieving competitive accuracy with orders of magnitude fewer parameters than prompt-based methods.

key_contributions.title: NuminaMath / AIMO Prize Winner
key_contributions.description: Teamed up with Numina to create an 850k math problem-solution dataset and won the first AI Math Olympiad progress prize, demonstrating the power of high-quality open datasets for mathematical reasoning.

key_contributions.title: SmolLM Series & Open-R1
key_contributions.description: Co-authored SmolLM2 and SmolLM3 small language models and co-launched the Open-R1 initiative to reproduce open reasoning model training, including OlympicCoder for competitive programming.

key_contributions.title: Hugging Face Course
key_contributions.description: Co-developed the free Hugging Face NLP course bridging software engineers into the Transformers ecosystem, covering tokenization, fine-tuning, and deployment across multiple modalities.

quotes.text: A few lines of code could outperform features that had been carefully designed by physicists over many years.
quotes.source: The Sequence Chat: Hugging Face's Lewis Tunstall on Zephyr, RLHF and LLM Innovation

quotes.text: You could skip the costly human annotation step altogether and focus on generating data for specific tasks.
quotes.source: The Sequence Chat: Hugging Face's Lewis Tunstall on Zephyr, RLHF and LLM Innovation

quotes.text: Just start. Just start coding. Just start contributing if you want to do open-source. You can always find reasons not to do it but you just have to get your hands dirty.
quotes.source: Machine Learning Experts Interview, Hugging Face Blog

quotes.text: The biggest lesson I learned when I was starting out in the field is using baseline models when starting out... you can actually get quite far with regular expressions and linear models like logistic regression.
quotes.source: Machine Learning Experts Interview, Hugging Face Blog

quotes.text: History generally shows that one shouldn't bet against deep learning!
quotes.source: The Sequence Chat: Lewis Tunstall on Building the Model that Won the AI Math Olympiad

quotes.text: Although there are several open weight models for mathematics, the training datasets are rarely, if ever, made public.
quotes.source: The Sequence Chat: Lewis Tunstall on Building the Model that Won the AI Math Olympiad
